D5 Design L4 Analyze Reference

Visual Weight & Balance

Arnheim's heuristic — size, contrast, and position give an element pull; find where a composition's weight actually centres

Arnheim argued a composition balances around visual weight, not geometry. Weight grows with an element's size and its contrast against the ground, and it is amplified by position: the top of a frame feels heavier than the bottom, and the right heavier than the left. The balance point is the weight centroid, not the geometric centre.

Move and resize the upper element; a second sits lower-left. Faint isobars show each one's field of pull. The gold mark is the weight centroid; the grey mark is the plain geometric midpoint — watch them separate as position and size shift the weight.
